title: Doll Bunk Beds for 18" Dolls
summary: A pocket-hole bunk bed scaled for 18" dolls, built from 1x2 and 1x1 stock with a full ladder and open-side guardrail — small enough to gift unfinished for a birthday paint job.
description:
This is a genuine bunk bed built at 18"-doll scale, from four tall 1x2 corner posts joined by rails at both bed levels, with a ladder on one end and a guardrail on the open side of the top bunk — the same structural pieces a full-size bunk bed has, just sized for a nightstand instead of a bedroom.

Build the two end assemblies first, using a scrap piece of 1x material as a spacer to keep the rail-to-post joints consistent — the same trick a full-size furniture build uses, just at a scale where a slightly-off spacer is much more visible. A pocket-hole jig keeps every joint's screw holes hidden inside the frame; countersunk screws driven from the outside work too, but need to be filled or covered afterward.

Glue and clamp before nailing — at this scale, clamps do far more of the real work than they would on full-size furniture, since there's very little material to hold a nail's grip on its own. The floor slats are thin plywood or hardboard scrap, which is a good use for the leftover cutoffs from almost any other build in this catalog.

A nice finishing option, and the one the original build used: leave the bed unfinished and gift it that way, so the birthday recipient can do their own painting rather than receiving a finished piece.

tools.note: Countersunk screws from the outside work too, filled or covered afterward.

materials.name: 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Corner posts, rails, guardrail, and floor slats.

materials.name: 1x1, 8 ft (or ripped from a 1x2)
materials.note: Ladder rails, ladder cap, and guardrail posts.

materials.name: Scrap hardboard, 1/4" plywood, or other sheet goods
materials.note: Floor slats or panel — any scrap qualifies.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws or 2" countersunk screws

cutList.note: The four tall posts shared by both bed levels.
Front and back rails at both the lower and upper bed levels, plus the ladder-side top rail.
Five per bed level, supporting the doll mattress.
Used as spacers/cleats during assembly per the source photos.

steps.title: Build the two end assemblies
steps.body: Build the two end frames from the corner posts and bed-level rails, using a scrap piece of 1x material as a spacer to keep the rail spacing consistent between the two ends. Attach with a pocket-hole jig, or with countersunk 2" screws from the outside if you don't have one.
materials: Pocket hole screws or 2" countersunk screws

steps.title: Join the two ends
steps.body: Join the two end assemblies together with the remaining bed-level rails, forming the full bunk frame.
materials: Pocket hole screws or 2" countersunk screws

steps.title: Add the ladder-side guardrail
steps.body: Attach the guardrail on the ladder side of the top bunk with the guardrail rails and posts.

steps.title: Add the reinforcing cleats
steps.body: Position, glue, and clamp the corner reinforcing cleats in place, then nail with 1-1/4" finish nails — clamps matter more at this scale than on full-size furniture, since there's little material for a nail to grip on its own.

steps.title: Add the floor slats
steps.body: Cut the scrap plywood or hardboard into floor slats or a single panel and set into each bed level to support the doll mattress. Any clean scrap works here.
materials: Scrap hardboard, 1/4" plywood, or other sheet goods

steps.title: Sand and finish (or leave unfinished)
steps.body: Sand every edge smooth. Finish with paint or stain, or gift the bed unfinished so the recipient can paint it themselves.

images.alt: Small wooden bunk bed sized for an 18-inch doll, with ladder and guardrail
